title: fix: pass image URLs directly to Pruna p-image-edit
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Skipping the middleman
gist.blurb: The Pruna image editor was getting confused by base64 data, so we stopped translating. We now hand it image URLs directly, which it vastly prefers.
gist.summary: Fixed an issue with the Pruna image editing model by passing image URLs directly instead of downloading and re-encoding them as base64 strings.
gist.impact: Image editing requests to the Pruna model now work reliably without throwing misleading input errors.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee bypassing a messy pile of base64 code to hand a clean, glowing URL link directly to the Monitor Robot, who is working on a digital painting.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot handing a glowing lime green (#ecf874) URL link directly to a chunky monitor-headed robot who is painting on a digital canvas. A messy pile of discarded base64 code scrolls sits in the corner. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
